As a part of this change, the ADC ECPD writer DB was bounced, and we noticed the Hawkeye alerts. Wallet location in listener.ora was wrong. It probably was a copy-paste from BDC. After changing that, the issue was resolved. This must have been like this from Nov 18th 2025. :oracle:ecpdpswr1:/oragrid/product/19c/grid/network/admin:$diff listener.ora.WithTDCWalletLocation listener.ora 58c58 < (DIRECTORY = /app/oracle/ewiptdcwtr-scan_wallet) — > (DIRECTORY = /app/oracle/ewipsdcwtr-scan_wallet) :oracle:ecpdpswr1:/oragrid/product/19c/grid/network/admin:$

CLOSURE_REMARKS: There was a connectivity issue overnight between Akamai DataStream and our ingestion endpoint. The logs piled up in a buffer. Since our dashboards use "Ingestion Time" (@AkamaiEventTime) instead of the actual "Event Time" (reqTimeSec), it stacked hours of traffic into a single 10-minute window. The data is all there, just plotted at the wrong time. The alerts will stop once the logs backlogs clears.

INCIDENT_REASON: Reason: 300% more traffic is observed compared to past window average traffic - 49605.0;System: B6NV ,DC: AWS-E ,MetricName: traffic ,Application: SOE-CPC-Services-Akamai-SITES
CLOSURE_REMARKS: There was a connectivity issue overnight between Akamai DataStream and our ingestion endpoint. The logs piled up in a buffer. Since our dashboards use "Ingestion Time" (@AkamaiEventTime) instead of the actual "Event Time" (reqTimeSec), it stacked hours of traffic into a single 10-minute window. The data is all there, just plotted at the wrong time. The alerts will stop once the logs backlogs clears.

INCIDENT_REASON: Reason: 300% more traffic is observed compared to past window average traffic - 27935.0;System: CV9V ,DC: AWS-E ,MetricName: traffic ,Application: SOE-Static-Akamai-ASSETS.example.com
CLOSURE_REMARKS: There was a connectivity issue overnight between Akamai DataStream and our ingestion endpoint. The logs piled up in a buffer. Since our dashboards use "Ingestion Time" (@AkamaiEventTime) instead of the actual "Event Time" (reqTimeSec), it stacked hours of traffic into a single 10-minute window. The data is all there, just plotted at the wrong time. The alerts will stop once the logs backlogs clears.
